# moderation

> Minimal MCP server for Rocket.Chat generated by rc-mcp.

**Generated by [rc-mcp](https://github.com/thekishandev/MCP-Server-Generator)** — Minimal MCP Server Generator for Rocket.Chat.

## Tools (7)

| Tool | Method | Endpoint | Description |
|------|--------|----------|-------------|
| `export_channel_history` | GET | `/api/v1/rooms.info` | Resolve a room by name and fetch its message history. Spans rooms + messaging. |
| `archive_channel` | GET | `/api/v1/rooms.info` | Resolve a channel by name and archive it. Combines lookup + archive into one operation. |
| `create_discussion_in_channel` | GET | `/api/v1/rooms.info` | Resolve a channel by name and create a threaded discussion in it. |
| `channels_info` | GET | `/api/v1/channels.info` | Get Channel Information. GET channels.info API wrapper. |
| `groups_history` | GET | `/api/v1/groups.history` | Get Group History. GET groups.history API wrapper. Requires: roomId. |
| `groups_archive` | POST | `/api/v1/groups.archive` | Archive a Group. POST groups.archive API wrapper. Requires: roomId. |
| `login` | POST | `/api/v1/login` | Login with Username and Password |

## Authentication

This server uses **auto-authentication from environment variables**. The `.env` file is pre-configured with your credentials — individual tools do NOT require `authToken` or `userId` params. Auth is handled automatically at startup.

Required env vars:
- **RC_URL** — your Rocket.Chat server URL
- **RC_AUTH_TOKEN** — your X-Auth-Token
- **RC_USER_ID** — your X-User-Id

## Quick Start

### 1. Install dependencies

\`\`\`bash
npm install
\`\`\`

### 2. Configure environment

\`\`\`bash
cp .env.example .env
# Only RC_URL is needed (defaults to http://localhost:3000)
\`\`\`

### 3. Build and run

\`\`\`bash
npm run build
npm start
\`\`\`

### 4. Use with gemini-cli

Add to your \`settings.json\`:

\`\`\`json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "moderation": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./dist/server.js"],
      "env": {
        "RC_URL": "http://localhost:3000"
      }
    }
  }
}
\`\`\`

Then just tell gemini-cli what you want to do:
\`\`\`
> send a message to channel #general
\`\`\`
The agent will ask for your Auth Token, User ID, and other required params.

## Development

\`\`\`bash
# Run in development mode (no build step)
npm run dev

# Run tests
npm test
\`\`\`
